title: SCL classifyInbound — Output Schema (v4)
description: Output of the locked v4 classifier. Returned via Anthropic structured outputs (output_config.format = json_schema). The TypeScript Zod definition lives in .sandbox/classify.mjs lines 33-83. Field-by-field meanings and integration guidance are in README.md.

classification.description: Overall bucket. NURTURE is the default for any non-HOT, non-DEAD, non-WRONG_NUMBER lead. Auto-flips to NURTURE when staleState='ghosted' (was HOT, 7+ days silent).

leadScore.description: 0-100 composite reflecting peak intent across the full thread, NOT current momentum. Locked in once HOT is reached. Can only decrease if the lead later expresses disqualifying signals.

revenueMonthly.description: USD monthly revenue if explicitly stated by the lead. null if not stated. NEVER inferred from annual.

revenueAnnual.description: USD annual revenue if explicitly stated. null if not stated. NEVER inferred from monthly.

revenueConfidence.description: high=explicit number; medium=range/approximate; low=vague (e.g. 'decent'); none=no signal.

amountRequested.description: USD dollar amount the lead explicitly named ('200-250k' → midpoint 225000). null if not stated.

useOfFunds.description: Short phrase if lead said what the money is for ('consolidate MCAs', 'tradeshow expenses', 'expansion'). null if not stated.

product.description: Best-fit product based on what the rep offered AND what the lead said. HELOC and 10/30-yr LOC dominate current data.

urgency.description: Time-sensitivity of the buying signal. 'high' for same-day urgency words, 'low' for stale/quiet, 'medium' otherwise.

objections.description: Up to 5 short phrases capturing what's blocking the deal ('behind on taxes', 'low FICO 500', 'stacked MCAs'). Empty array if none.

suggestedReply.description: ONE SMS the rep can send AS-IS to advance the lead. Under 300 chars. Tone-matched. Never promises approval/rates/amounts.

suggestedFollowupTime.description: REQUIRED when classification='HOT'. Natural phrase ('in 2 hours', 'tomorrow 9am') or ISO timestamp. null for non-HOT.

suggestedFollowupReason.description: REQUIRED when classification='HOT'. One short sentence explaining the timing choice. null for non-HOT.

staleState.description: Measured by days since the LEAD's last inbound (NOT since rep's last outbound). null=not HOT and never was. fresh=HOT, lead-last-inbound ≤2 days. stale=HOT, 2-7 days. ghosted=was HOT, 7+ days silent (auto-flips classification to NURTURE).

hadMeaningfulEngagement.description: True if the lead replied to outbound at any point with substantive content (acknowledgment, email, yes, real text). Marker that the lead is reactivatable. False only for never-replied, auto-bounces, gibberish, or pure opt-outs. Used as the trigger gate for re-engage messages.

suggestedReengageMessage.description: REQUIRED (string) when staleState='stale' OR staleState='ghosted' OR (hadMeaningfulEngagement=true AND lead's last inbound ≥7 days ago). References the prior conversation explicitly. null otherwise. Different from suggestedReply — this is the message to send AFTER a silence gap, not the next inline reply.

repBehavior.description: good=professional/on-script. concerning=typos/mild pushiness/missed signal/awkward phrasing. poor=rude/abrasive/wrong-name/promised approval/profanity. Does NOT pull down the lead score.

coachingNote.description: Empty string when repBehavior='good'. Otherwise ONE sentence on what the rep should change. Manager-facing, not customer-facing.

reasoning.description: ONE sentence explaining the classification. Diagnostic only — for prompt debugging. NEVER surface to reps or customers.

$comment: Conditional requirements (not expressed as JSON Schema dependencies — enforced by the prompt and validated client-side): (1) suggestedFollowupTime + suggestedFollowupReason MUST be non-null when classification='HOT' and SHOULD be null otherwise. (2) suggestedReengageMessage MUST be non-null when staleState IN ('stale','ghosted') OR (hadMeaningfulEngagement=true AND lead's last inbound ≥7 days ago). (3) staleState='ghosted' MUST coexist with classification='NURTURE' (auto-flip). (4) WRONG_NUMBER + DEAD typically have hadMeaningfulEngagement=false.
